skill.name: vanilla-webpage
skill.description: Guides agents to build static webpages with vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, using modern CSS, accessible patterns, templates, and cookbook examples without a build step.
skill.summary: Create no-build static webpages with vanilla HTML, CSS, JavaScript, design tokens, and accessible cookbook patterns.
skill.author: yuzu-octopus

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many hits from Markdown backticks, CSS values, example URLs, and documentation text that are false positives. I confirmed three browser-side XSS risks where examples teach or implement innerHTML rendering of user-provided content without escaping.

remediation.issue: Cookbook todo example renders user-controlled todo text with innerHTML.
remediation.suggestion: Build list items with createElement and assign todo text through textContent, or sanitize user content before rendering.

remediation.issue: Full-project notes tutorial renders note text with innerHTML.
remediation.suggestion: Replace template-string HTML rendering with DOM node creation and textContent for note text and button labels.

remediation.issue: The main skill documentation shows innerHTML template rendering without a clear safety warning at the example.
remediation.suggestion: Add an explicit warning near the example and show a safe DOM-construction alternative for untrusted text.

4. **Native platform feature covers it?** `<dialog>` for modals, `<details>` for accordions, CSS ove
- **Reference cookbook:** Browse `cookbook/` before writing new CSS from scratch
| Modal (blocks page) | `<dialog>` | Focus trap, `::backdrop`, ESC to close |
| Disclosure (optional visibility) | `<details>/<summary>` | No JS, semantic, SEO-friendly |
| Non-modal tooltip/menu | `[popover]` | Lightweight, no focus trap, positioning |
Prefer HTML5 native validation (`required`, `pattern`, `type`) for simple cases. Use JS validation o
**Rules:** Use semantic HTML first. `role` is rarely needed (buttons, links, navigation have implici
- Declarative open/close for `<dialog>` and `<popover>` without JavaScript
> Use `light-dark()` for auto-only theme (respects OS). Use `data-theme` attribute toggle when user
- Parameters start with `--` like custom properties
- `result` defines the return value (not `return` like JS)
- Use `span N` instead of hardcoded line numbers for responsive grids
- Works with SPA navigation and MPA with `@view-transition`
- The `to` clause limits scope to a boundary element
- Use `<template>` + `cloneNode` for reusable DOM fragments with event listeners. Use template liter
- Loaded via single `<link>` tag from CDN (or minimal setup)
| `npm init` / `package.json` for static HTML | Just write HTML files |
| `src/` / `dist/` folder "for later" | Root-level files until there's a need |
| `<script>` in `<head>` without `defer` | `<script defer>` or place before `</body>` |
| `innerHTML` with user input | `textContent` or sanitize with DOMPurify |
| onclick in template literals | Event delegation with `data-id` attributes (§6) |
Patterns live in `cookbook/` — each is a standalone HTML file with embedded CSS. Browse before writi

- Children inherit parent grid tracks instead of creating their own

high_findings.title: innerHTML assignment (XSS risk)
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The todo example inserts user-controlled todo text from localStorage into innerHTML without escaping. A copied example can execute injected markup in the browser.

high_findings.title: innerHTML assignment (XSS risk)
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The notes tutorial renders note text collected from an input into innerHTML without sanitization. The surrounding tutorial context confirms this is user-controlled browser content.

medium_findings.title: innerHTML assignment (XSS risk)
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill documents a template-literal rendering pattern that writes item fields through innerHTML without an adjacent sanitization warning. It is documentation, but agents may copy it into pages with user-controlled data.

content.value_statement: Simple webpages often do not need a build system or frontend framework. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create accessible static pages with modern HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
actual_capabilities: Selects vanilla HTML, CSS, JavaScript, or a small allowed framework based on task complexity.
Provides templates for blank pages, token-based starters, and classless framework pages.
Includes cookbook examples for cards, forms, navigation, layouts, effects, and interactive components.
Documents accessibility checks for semantic structure, focus states, labels, contrast, and reduced motion.
Guides responsive layouts with grid, flexbox, clamp, container queries, and modern CSS features.
Supports visual verification workflows using a local static server and browser inspection tools.
limitations: It is intended for static pages and small interactive experiences, not full application frameworks.
It does not provide a backend, database, authentication service, or deployment pipeline.
Some cookbook JavaScript examples are demonstrations and need hardening before production use.
Framework documentation may require external lookup when the latest API details matter.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Product Page
use_cases.description: Create a responsive marketing or product page with semantic sections, polished CSS, and no build tooling.

use_cases.title: Build a Documentation Page
use_cases.description: Generate readable documentation layouts with navigation, typography, code blocks, and accessible structure.

use_cases.title: Create a Small Browser Tool
use_cases.description: Build a single-page utility with form controls, local interactivity, and clear responsive behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Landing Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a first static page with no tooling.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a single-file static webpage for a small business. Use semantic HTML, responsive CSS, accessible colors, and no build step.

prompt_templates.title: Responsive Layout
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve structure across mobile and desktop.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a responsive webpage layout with a sticky header, feature sections, cards, and a footer. Use vanilla CSS and explain any layout decisions briefly.

prompt_templates.title: Interactive Form
prompt_templates.scenario: Add browser-side interaction while staying framework-free.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an accessible contact form with validation states, keyboard-friendly controls, and safe DOM updates using vanilla JavaScript.

prompt_templates.title: Polished Multi-Section Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Combine cookbook patterns with design tokens.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the vanilla webpage skill to create a polished multi-section dashboard page. Reuse cookbook patterns where appropriate and verify responsive behavior.

output_examples.input: Create a no-build homepage for a local design studio.
output_examples.output: A single HTML page with semantic sections, embedded styles, responsive typography, accessible buttons, and clear visual hierarchy.

output_examples.input: Build a small notes interface using browser storage.
output_examples.output: A static page with form controls, saved notes, edit and delete actions, keyboard support, and safe text rendering guidance.

output_examples.input: Make a documentation page with navigation and examples.
output_examples.output: A readable documentation layout with a sidebar, article sections, code examples, responsive behavior, and accessible focus styles.

best_practices: Start with semantic HTML and add CSS or JavaScript only when the page behavior requires it.
Use the provided tokens and cookbook patterns before inventing new spacing, color, or component styles.
Render user-provided text with textContent or sanitized output instead of raw innerHTML.
anti_patterns: Adding a package manager, build tool, or large framework for a simple static page.
Copying cookbook JavaScript into production without checking input handling and accessibility.
Using visual effects that reduce readability, contrast, keyboard access, or motion comfort.

faq.question: Does this skill require Node or npm?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed for static HTML, CSS, and JavaScript served directly by a simple local server.

faq.question: Can it use CSS frameworks?
faq.answer: Yes, but only small allowed frameworks when the decision framework shows they are useful for the page.

faq.question: Is it suitable for complex web applications?
faq.answer: No. It is best for static pages, prototypes, documentation pages, and small browser tools.

faq.question: Does it include accessibility guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers semantic markup, labels, keyboard focus, contrast, reduced motion, and responsive behavior.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill declares support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Are the examples production ready?
faq.answer: They are useful starting points. JavaScript examples that render user input should be reviewed and hardened before production use.
